methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Salient Motion is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $12M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public company, spanning Series A through Series B stages. The private peer set includes 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Foundation Robotics ($3.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M), among others.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using $12M in total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $24M. The peer-median approach anchors to the median valuation of 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables at $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $20M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $837M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty regarding Salient Motion's competitive positioning, technology differentiation, and near-term revenue trajectory relative to a peer set that exhibits significant stage and maturity heterogeneity. The stage-progression method yields a floor estimate, while peer multiples anchor an upper bound; convergence depends on additional clarity on unit economics and market traction.
